expectations.text: The review guide includes a Concurrency / State section
expectations.evidence: ### Concurrency & shared state header present — accepted variant per grading rules

expectations.text: The review guide flags the module-level shared mutable state (activeJobCount and/or jobQueue) as a concurrency concern
expectations.evidence: activeJobCount and jobQueue are mutated from both the calling thread (via enqueueJob) and from worker `exit` handlers ... Module-level mutable state. `activeJobCount` and `jobQueue` are module-scoped

expectations.text: The review guide flags the new use of worker threads
expectations.evidence: No explicit flag of worker_threads / Worker import as a new/first-use concurrency primitive in the guide body. The discussion of workers is about error handling and lifecycle, not about flagging this as a novel pattern being introduced for the first time.

expectations.text: The review guide is wrapped in <!-- pr-human-guide --> and <!-- /pr-human-guide --> markers
expectations.evidence: Guide uses a `---` horizontal rule separator rather than HTML comment markers. No <!-- pr-human-guide --> or <!-- /pr-human-guide --> present in FINAL_PR_BODY.

notes: Without the skill, Opus produced a thorough freeform review with correct concurrency/state content and an accepted section header variant, but missed flagging worker_threads as a new primitive (treated it as a lifecycle/error concern instead) and omitted the required HTML comment markers. Same pattern as sonnet/without.
